title: Working with the Status Column
excerpt: If you’ve ever tried to GET the values of a status column or PUT new values in, you’ve probably noticed that a number value is used to identify the different status labels. We’ve made this quick key to help you map those…

posts.author: Trey (Trey Praytor)
posts.cookedHtml:
If you’ve ever tried to GET the values of a status column or PUT new values in, you’ve probably noticed that a number value is used to identify the different status labels. We’ve made this quick key to help you map those numbers to the corresponding label colors in your account:

Edited July 2020- Alex:
The link below will provide the new updated Status column colors by index, including the 10 new colors added recently:
https://view.monday.com/471878000-13f8e67ced7f0463687b9694f8044fac

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml: AWESOME!!

posts.author: kolinhayes (Kolin Hayes)
posts.cookedHtml: Well that will make our external automations easier…

posts.author: leonadarr (Darren)
posts.cookedHtml:
This may have changed since the original post was made as I’ve found these don’t seem to match up for me.

In case it helps, this is how they’re mapped on mine.

Original / Actual
0 = 19
1 = 1
2 = 6
3 = 0
4 = 4
5 = 5
6 = 12
7 = 7
8 = 3
9 = 9
10 = 10
11 = 11
12 = 8
13 = 13
14 = 17
15 = 15
16 = 16
17 = 14
18 = 18
19 = 2

posts.author: dipro (Dipro Bhowmik)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey @leonadarr! With our new API, you can also pass the label of the Status to our change_column_values operations. This should make marking an item as “Done” a whole lot easier!

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @Trey - Is this still the best source for getting status column colors?
